observation.summary: SaaS assignment docs mixed implemented daemon behavior, upstream/client boundaries, and a stale /thread-progress reporter claim.

hypothesis.summary: A manifest can generate reader and migration docs, prove source checks, and fail when daemon code claims forbidden control-plane/client surfaces.

execution.summary: Run saasassignment to check generated docs, local source evidence, absent surfaces, and compact evidence output.

evaluation.summary: The verifier rejects generated doc drift, source drift, unknown source references, implemented facts without evidence, and forbidden /thread-progress or private server surfaces.

retrospective.summary: SaaS assignment polling knowledge becomes executable evidence and no longer lets client/control-plane ownership drift into daemon docs.

evidence.proves: SaaS assignment source manifest matches generated docs, source checks, and absent surface rules
public CI uploads SaaS assignment source evidence
